mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) positively predicts S&P 500 direction over the next 20 trading days because retail sales are a coincident indicator of consumer demand that directly feeds into corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward hit rate of positive directional forecasts is below 55% during periods of RSAFS growth, or Rank IC is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE below 7.5% following a sustained period above that level triggers a repricing of recession risk downward, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors discount a stronger labor market recovery.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% after 3 consecutive months above 7.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held below 0.25% while CPIAUCSL registers year-over-year inflation above 1.5%, deeply negative real rates drive capital out of fixed income and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 45% of observations, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exhibits a positive rather than negative correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal accelerating residential investment that amplifies GDP growth during post-crisis recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: During post-crisis recovery regimes identified by GDPC1 below trend and HOUST rising, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months, or the linear projection of returns on HOUST yields a negative coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) captures rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into near-term earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 direction over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The directional accuracy of INDPRO momentum for 5-day SPX returns falls below 52% over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean return following positive INDPRO surprises is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in FEDFUNDS above 0.25% sustained for two consecutive months marks the end of zero-interest-rate policy, raising the discount rate on future cash flows and increasing corporate borrowing costs, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the average return during the first three months of such tightening episodes is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market that boosts aggregate income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings expansion.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases MoM and UNRATE decreases MoM
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the joint signal's information coefficient is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized growth in real GDP (GDPC1) exceeding 2.0% confirms the economy is operating above stall speed, which reduces left-tail recession risk and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days via multiple expansion.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth > 2.0% are negative in more than 45% of observations, or the average forward return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
